comment: Bullet jump (cf. Warframe): from a CROUCH or SLIDE, a directional burst that launches the player forward (along aim/look = the `forward` space) AND up. Triggered by the SAME `jump` input (buffered) while in a slide or crouch — exactly the Warframe idiom. Priority 95 supersedes the base kit's plain slide-jump / crouch-jump (which fired on `jump` at priority 90 and 90 respectively); those base transitions have been removed so there is no unreachable duplicate and no (state, priority) collision. Pure DATA: composed onto base.kit.json via kit overlay; uses ONLY existing Condition/Effect primitives (input_buffered, timer, all; add_velocity(forward), set_velocity_y, set_collider_height, set_timer). A cooldown timer (bullet_jump_cd) is the guard, expressed entirely as data. NO ENGINE CODE CHANGE. See docs/decisions/movement-substrate.md §2 (composition), Slice 4.

SLIDE.comment: Bullet jump out of a slide: jump input while in SLIDE. Priority 95 — the base kit's plain slide-jump (priority 90) has been removed so this is the sole jump-from-slide handler, with no race or unreachable duplicate. Stands up (with headroom), bursts forward+up along aim, arms the cooldown.

CROUCH.comment: Bullet jump out of a crouch: jump input while in CROUCH. Priority 95 — the base kit's plain crouch-jump (priority 90) has been removed so this is the sole jump-from-crouch handler, with no race or unreachable duplicate. Stands up, bursts forward+up along aim, arms the cooldown.
